title: Shopify AI Store Builder needed for team
description:
We are an eCommerce company looking for a talented and efficient Shopify Store Builder & Operator who can build high-quality, branded Shopify stores using AI tools — with limited resources and fast turnaround times.



This is not a basic theme install job. We need someone who can take a product, a brand name, and minimal assets — and build a fully branded, conversion-optimized Shopify store from scratch using AI for copywriting, product images, and store content. You will be expected to operate at a high level across every part of the store build.



---



REQUIRED — REVIEW THIS EXAMPLE BEFORE APPLYING 



Study this store carefully. This is the standard of quality we expect you to deliver:







Pay attention to the product page structure, AI-generated product images, infographic-style PDP images, benefit sections, customer testimonial layouts, comparison tables, results timelines, FAQ sections, trust badges, and overall branding. If you cannot build a store at this level, please do not apply.



---



THE ROLE



You are an overall Shopify store operator and builder. You will be responsible for the entire store — from theme setup and customization to product images, copywriting, app integrations, and final QA. You must have a deep understanding of Shopify and how to build stores that look professional and convert.



---



AI TOOLS WE USE — YOU MUST BE COMFORTABLE WITH THESE



- Nano Banana Pro — for generating product images, lifestyle shots, infographic visuals, and branded PDP imagery when we have limited or no photography. You will be creating professional product images from scratch using AI.

- Claude Opus 4.6 — for writing all store copy including product descriptions, headlines, benefit sections, FAQ content, comparison copy, and homepage content. You must know how to prompt Claude effectively to get high-converting, brand-appropriate copy.

- You should have a deep understanding of how to use AI as your primary tool for building stores with limited resources. We are not handing you a full brand kit with professional photography — you are building the brand visually and verbally using AI.



---



REQUIREMENTS



- Proven experience building Shopify stores from scratch or redesigning existing ones — with examples

- Deep expertise with Shrine Pro theme specifically — this is the theme we use across our stores

- Ability to generate professional, branded product images using Nano Banana Pro or similar AI image tools when no photography exists

- Strong copywriting skills OR the ability to use Claude Opus 4.6 effectively to produce compelling product descriptions, headlines, benefit copy, FAQs, and full page content

- Knowledge of Shopify apps and integrations — you must be able to set up and configure apps such as Loox (reviews), 
, upsell apps, subscription apps,
 marketing integrations, and other conversion-focused tools

- Understanding of conversion-optimized store layouts — including trust badges, urgency elements, social proof sections, comparison tables, ingredient/benefit breakdowns, and results timelines

- Ability to build stores that look and feel like the Elare example above — branded, professional, and high-converting even with limited starting assets

- Must be able to deliver projects quickly without compromising quality

- Fluent in English, both written and spoken

- Strong attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines



---



YOUR TASKS AS A SHOPIFY STORE BUILDER & OPERATOR



- Build complete Shopify stores from scratch using Shrine Pro theme

- Customize theme sections, layouts, and styling to match each brand

- Generate all product images using Nano Banana Pro — including main product shots, lifestyle images, infographic PDP images, and benefit visuals

- Write or generate all store copy using Claude Opus 4.6 — product pages, homepage, about page, FAQ, and any additional content sections

- Set up navigation, collections, product pages, and variant structures

- Install and configure Shopify apps — Loox, 
, upsell/cross-sell apps, subscription tools,
 capture, and other functionality-enhancing apps

- Build out trust and conversion elements — money-back guarantee sections, comparison tables, ingredient breakdowns, testimonial carousels, and results timelines

- Ensure the store is fully functional, mobile-optimized, and ready to launch

- Handle revisions and feedback to refine design and content



---



QUESTIONS TO ANSWER



1. How many Shopify stores have you built? Please share live examples or screenshots.

2. Are you experienced with Shrine Pro theme specifically? If so, show examples.

3. Have you used Nano Banana Pro or similar AI image generation tools to create product images for Shopify stores? Please share examples of AI-generated product imagery you've created.

4. Have you used Claude (or similar AI tools) for writing store copy — product descriptions, benefit sections, FAQs, etc.? Describe how you use it in your workflow.

5. Walk us through your process for building a branded store when you're given only a product, a brand name, and minimal assets. How do you go from zero to a finished store?

6. Which Shopify apps have you worked with? Are you comfortable setting up Loox, 
, subscription apps, upsell tools, and similar integrations?

7. How do you ensure fast turnaround times without compromising on quality?

8. Can you look at the Elare store example linked above and confidently say you can build at that level? Be honest.

9. Are you comfortable handling revisions and feedback to improve store design and content?



---



COMPENSATION



$50 CAD per store build



This is a part-time position, but we are looking for someone available for long-term collaboration with ongoing projects. We are scaling quickly and will have consistent store builds coming through.



---



*** HOW TO APPLY ***



*You must include your favorite Shopify theme in the subject line of your application for it to be considered (this shows you actually read it and is very important).*



1.) Send us a message answering all 9 questions

2.) Include examples of Shopify stores you've built — specifically stores where you used AI tools for product images and/or copywriting

3.) Share any AI-generated product images you've created using Nano Banana Pro or similar tools

4.) Provide your contact information (WhatsApp, etc.)



---



If you are a strong Shopify store builder who understands how to use AI tools like Nano Banana Pro and Claude Opus 4.6 to build branded, conversion-optimized stores with limited resources — and you can deliver at the quality level shown in our Elare example — we want to hear from you.



If you cannot build at that standard, this is not the right fit. We need someone who can take minimal inputs and deliver a polished, launch-ready store.

title: Assistant Operations Manager - Remote| Singapore Company
description:
ASSISTANT OPERATIONS MANAGER — REMOTE | SINGAPORE COMPANY

Slim Sassy Strong Pte Ltd | Remote Full-Time | php20K–45K+ | Singapore travel opportunities



You’re good at your job. You’re just tired of the commute.

You’ve spent years working onsite — handling clients, managing workflows, keeping things running. You’re competent, you’re professional, and you show up.

But the traffic is costing you 2–3 hours every day. Hours you’d rather spend building something.

We’re offering you a way out of the commute — without stepping back in your career.



Slim Sassy Strong is a women’s wellness education company headquartered in Singapore. We are hiring one Assistant Operations Manager to join our remote operations team, with a structured path to Operations Manager and Senior Operations Manager.



This is a serious career role, not a work-from-home side gig.



|Phase   |Role                     |Compensation    |

|--------|-------------------------|----------------|

|Month 1 |Training                 |php20,000 fixed   |

|Phase 2 |AOM (KPI-based)          |php28,000 starting|

|Phase 3 |Operations Manager       |php35,000–php45,000 |

|Advanced|Senior Operations Manager|php45,000+        |



Plus: selected tea
mbers travel to Singapore for company events and operations.

Advancement is based on performance — not time served.



You will work directly with Singapore-based leadership as an operational extension of the business:

	-	Manage executive calendars and protect leadership time

	-	Own structured
 communication and client follow-ups

	-	Track KPIs, deadlines, and team task execution

	-	Conduct client-facing calls — professionally and confidently

	-	Support the consult pipeline and marketing-to-ops handover

	-	Maintain SOP compliance and operational documentation



As you grow into OM, you lead the team, own systems, and make decisions independently.

Client communication is a core part of this role — not an occasional task.



Why this works for you:

	-	No commute. Work from home, full-time, with a Singapore company on your resume.

	-	Real career track — AOM ? OM ? SOM with compensation that grows with your scope

	-	Singapore travel — top performers join us onsite for events and operational stints

	-	Above-market ceiling — ?45K+ for senior performers

	-	Long-term stability — we grow with people who perform; we don’t churn talent



You’ll do well here if:

You’ve worked onsite in operations, client coordination, admin, hospitality, or a similar field. You’re used to being relied on. You’re not looking for an easy job — you’re looking for a better setup to do your best work in.

Specifically, you:

	-	Are confident handling clients directly — calls, not just chat

	-	Stay composed and professional under pressure

	-	Meet deadlines without being chased

	-	Communicate clearly and directly, especially when escalating

	-	Are fast with digital tools and willing to learn new systems

	-	Want to build a career, not just collect a paycheck

	-	You don’t mind putting in extra hours when it counts. You just want those hours to actually mean something.



Technical requirements

	-	Minimum 50 WPM typing speed

	-	Strong written and spoken English — business standard

	-	Stable internet with a documented backup connection

	-	Own laptop suitable for business use

	-	Familiarity with Google Workspace or similar tools



How to apply:



 with the exact subject line:

Assistant Operations Manager Application

Include all of the following — incomplete applications will not be reviewed:

	1.	One paragraph: what you’ve handled onsite and why you’re ready to bring that to a remote leadership role

	2.	Your current city and power/internet stability details

	3.	Device specs + internet speed screenshot

	4.	Your backup plan during outages

	5.	A 1–2 minute informal audio introduction

	6.	DISC profile screenshot (OnlineJobs)

	7.	Typing test screenshot (min. 50 WPM)

title: Executive Assistant
description:
EXECUTIVE ASSISTANT (Female only, Full-Time, Remote – Philippines)



Join a mission-driven company empowering women across Asia to live stronger, healthier, and happier lives.

We’re growing fast — and we’re looking for a highly organised, proactive, and loyal Executive Assistant to support our leadership team and keep daily operations running like clockwork. 



How it looks like to be on this team: 



This is a long-term, full-time position (not freelance) with a company that values your growth, integrity, and professional excellence. 



???? YOUR ROLE



You’ll work directly with our senior leadership team, helping coordinate schedules, manage communications, and streamline key projects.



Your main responsibilities:



* Manage daily schedules, calendars, and appointments

* Handle professional
s — screen, prioritise, and draft replies

* Track projects, tasks, and documentation accurately

* Conduct research and prepare executive reports or presentations

* Coordinate between departments (Ops, Sales, Coaches, HR)

* Assist with HR, onboarding, and internal communications

* Manage travel logistics and event planning (as needed)

* Uphold confidentiality and professionalism at all times



*** WHAT YOU’LL LOVE ABOUT WORKING WITH US



* 95% Remote Work — eliminate long commutes, enjoy more balance

* Competitive KPI-based pay— reward tied to real performance

* Stable, long-term role — this is a career path, not a gig

* Clear SOPs & structured onboarding to set you up for success

* Supportive leadership that values feedback and transparency

* Collaborative, growth-driven culture — your voice matters here

* Training & development — we invest in your continuous learning

* Respectful, integrity-first workplace



*** YOU’RE A GREAT FIT IF YOU HAVE:



* Excellent English communication skills— written & spoken

* Typing speed of at least 45 WPM

* Strong organisational and problem-solving skills

* Experience with Google Workspace, MS Office, and online tools

* Proactive attitude and impeccable attention to detail

* Reliable internet connection with backup plan

* Quiet, professional home workspace with webcam and lighting

* Prior experience as Executive Assistant / Admin / VA is a plus

* A personality grounded in honesty, initiative, and consistency



*** NOT A GOOD FIT IF YOU:



* Have other part
 work

* Unfamiliar with basic remote work tech set up (google suite, zoom etc)

* Struggle with deadlines or frequent absences

* Need constant reminders or supervision

* Complain when things get challenging instead of problem-solving

* Lack a stable workspace or internet connection



We need someone who shows up, communicates clearly, and takes ownership.



*** WORK HOURS



Tuesday to Saturday, 1:00 PM – 10:00 PM (Singapore time)

This ensures smooth coordination with our Singapore-based team.



*** OUR COMPANY CULTURE



We live by five core values that define everything we do:



* Honesty — tell the truth, always.

* Integrity — deliver with excellence and accuracy.

* Commitment — follow through on your word.

* Growth Mindset — keep learning, keep improving.

* Team Spirit — work with respect, collaboration, and positivity.



*** HOW TO APPLY



Send an
 with the subject line: “Personal Executive Assistant” and include clear answers to ALL of the following:



1. Why should we hire you? Describe your experience — don’t list it.

2. Where in the Philippines are you based, and how stable is your power/internet?

3. What equipment do you use (computer specs, internet speed)?

4. What’s your plan if there’s a power or internet outage?

5. Record a short audio intro — share your name, favourite food, and favourite place to visit!

6. Attach a screenshot of your DISC profile from OnlineJobs.ph.

7. Take a typing test from 
  and include your screenshot result.



*** READY TO GROW WITH US?



If you’re seeking long-term stability, a positive and structured team culture, and the chance to grow in a meaningful role — we’d love to hear from you.



Apply now and become part of a company that values excellence, growth, and integrity. 



Warm regards

Terry Lim

HR Manager

title: Xero Setup Specialist & Trainer (Expert Level – Remote, Philippines)
description:
We are seeking an exceptional Xero expert to lead the complete setup, optimisation, and training process for our accounting systems.



This is not a basic bookkeeping role. We are looking for someone who deeply understands Xero architecture, financial workflows, reporting structures, and best-practice configuration — and who can confidently teach and guide a business owner in using it effectively.



???? About the Role



You will be responsible for:



Conducting a full audit of our current Xero setup (or leading a fresh setup if required)

Designing and implementing a best-practice chart of accounts

Structuring tracking categories aligned with business operations

Optimising bank feeds, automation rules, and reconciliations

Setting up invoices, bills, payroll (if applicable), and expense workflows

Configuring financial reporting dashboards for decision-making

Ensuring clean, scalable systems aligned with business growth

In addition to setup, you will:

Train and coach the founder on how to confidently use Xero

Create simple SOPs and Loom videos where helpful

Provide structured walkthrough sessions (live training)

Answer ongoing technical and workflow questions

Recommend improvements proactively

This is both a technical implementation and education role.



???? Required Experience



3+ years working extensively inside Xero

Proven experience setting up Xero accounts from scratch

Strong understanding of:

Chart of accounts structure

Tracking categories

Reporting & financial statements

Bank reconciliation best practices

Automation rules & integrations

Experience working with Australian businesses (highly preferred)

Excellent English communication skills (written and verbal)

Ability to explain financial systems clearly and confidently



???? Ideal Candidate



You are:



Extremely detail-oriented

Process-driven and organised

Proactive in suggesting improvements

Comfortable teaching and guiding non-accountants

Strategic in how you structure financial systems

Reliable and responsive

You don’t just “use Xero” — you understand how to architect it properly.



???? Scope & Engagement



Remote position (Philippines-based)



Initial project: Full Xero audit & optimisation



Ongoing: Training + advisory support



Flexible hours, but must overlap partially with Australian time zones



Potential for long-term working relationship



???? To Apply



Please include:



A brief overview of your Xero experience



Examples of Xero setups you have implemented



Your experience working with Australian businesses (if applicable)



Your hourly rate



A short Loom video explaining how you would approach setting up Xero for a growing service-based business



We are looking for someone exceptional — not average.



If you are a true Xero expert who enjoys building clean systems and teaching others how to use them properly, we would love to hear from you.

title: Killer Shopify Manager
description:
We’re looking for a young gun who knows Shopify properly. And please, read the section at the bottom of this page, about who we DONT want.



Someone sharp. Fast. Hungry. Someone who actually likes building stores, fixing problems, improving pages, making things convert better, and learning how real growth works inside a fast-moving agency.



You need to be genuinely good at Shopify.

That part matters most.



We want someone who:



knows their way around Shopify day to day, and code

can handle pages, apps, theme edits, store setup, and general backend management

has energy

wants to learn more

wants to work with other ambitious young people

wants to level up hard



This is for someone who wants more than a basic job.

You’ll work inside The Cultivator with a young team, get exposed to serious growth work, build real skills, and be around people chasing big things.



The kind of person we want probably sees themselves doing very well in life. Nice cars. Big future. Real 
ntum.

But right now, they’re ready to earn it.



If you’re switched on, confident with Shopify, and keen to grow with a killer agency, we want to hear from you.



Who We’re Not Looking For:



We’re not looking for someone who’s been sitting in the industry for years and has picked up slow habits, stale thinking, or a big ego from doing the same thing too long.



We’re also not looking for someone whose whole skillset starts and ends with Canva. That’s not enough here. This role needs someone who actually understands Shopify and can work inside a store properly.



And we’re definitely not looking for low-energy people. If you’re flat, slow to move, hard to motivate, or just want to coast, this won’t be the place for you.



We want someone fresh, switched on, sharp, and ready to get after it.

title: Job Title: Presentation Designer — AI Sponsor Pack (Urgent / Short-Term Contract)
description:
Overview

We are seeking a designer to work with us on refining an AI Sponsor Pack. This is a priority piece with a tight turnaround, so we need someone strong in conceptual, strategic design — not just formatting.



Objective

Translate a complex, text-heavy deck into a clear, visually-led narrative that:



Lands quickly with corporate sponsors

Makes abstract concepts tangible and compelling

Elevates the overall polish and credibility of the work



This is not a redesign from scratch — it's about refining and reinterpreting key slides.



Scope of Work

Focus on Slides 2–16 of the current master pack. Key tasks:



Simplify dense content into clear visual hierarchies

Translate complex ideas into conceptual diagrams and frameworks

Introduce strong visual storytelling (not decorative design)

Create consistency in layout, flow, and visual language

Improve readability and sponsor appeal



Design Direction

We need someone who can:



Think strategically (not just execute)

Translate complexity into simple, powerful visuals

Work with abstract/system-level concepts (AI, leadership, ethics)

Balance credibility, sophistication, and clarity



Style reference:



Clean, minimal, high-end

Conceptual (e.g. systems maps, frameworks, flows)

Confident use of space and hierarchy

Aligned with Menzies brand





Key Content to Bring to Life

The designer will need to help translate concepts such as:



"Symbiotic leadership" (human + AI systems)

"AI Ethical Advisor" (product concept)

"Universal basic infrastructure" (north star — currently abstract)

System-level ecosystem / network thinking



These are currently too text-heavy and need visual articulation.



Outputs



Refined slides (2–16) in Canva

Visual frameworks/diagrams embedded within slides

Consistent visual system that can be applied across future decks



Timing



Start: ASAP (ideally within 24–48 hours)



What We Are Looking For

Please apply only if you:



Have experience with strategy decks / consulting-style presentations

Can show examples of conceptual visualisation (not just branding)

Are comfortable working quickly and collaboratively



Materials Provided



Master deck (Canva)

Strategic input and direction from our team

title: Executive Assistant / Founder Ops Coordinator
description:
TLDR: Organize chaos. Build systems. Help a high-output founder scale intelligently.



We'll start at $1,400-1,900/month, and move to $2,000-2,500 for those who demonstrate founder-fit, project management / SOP skills, excellent English, financial experience (management or analysis/advisory roles), and real proactive-judgement when it comes to prioritization around our bottom-line.





The short version:



I’m hiring a highly capable Executive Assistant / Founder Operations Coordinator to help me get organized, protect my time, and turn a fast-moving founder-led business into a far more structured, scalable operation. It's already very structured, but fragmented.



This is not a basic virtual assistant role.



I’m not looking for someone to just manage a calendar and answer
s. I’m looking for someone who can help turn complexity into clarity, distill messy real-world workflows into SOPs, and become a force multiplier inside a business built around systems, automation, research, outbound infrastructure, and high-trust execution.



I move fast. I build quickly. I see patterns early. I can connect dots across markets, tools, people, and opportunities. What I need is someone exceptional at capturing, organizing, documenting, prioritizing, and operationalizing what’s already happening — so the business can scale without everything living in my head.



If you thrive on organizing chaos for a mad scientist / lifelong founder & entrepreneur, keep reading.





About me and what you’re walking into:



I’m a founder/operator building systems around:



* compliance-first growth infrastructure

* research and relationship-driven business development

* AI and automation (since 2012)

* outbound and CRM workflows

* process design and operational leverage



I’m very good at building things, spotting opportunities, architecting systems, and moving with speed.



I am much less interested in:



* repetitive admin

* maintaining pristine structure for its own sake

* manually keeping track of every moving part once the machine gets complex

* slowing down to document everything as I go



That creates an obvious gap:

there is often more capability in the business than organization around it.



The right person will help close that gap.



You will not be stepping into a boring, heavily managed corporate role. You will be stepping into a founder-led environment with a lot of moving pieces, a lot of 
ntum, and a lot of room to create order, leverage, and calm.





What this role actually is:



This role sits somewhere between:



* executive assistant

* operator

* project coordinator

* SOP/documentation lead

* founder-side integrator



At first, your job is simple to describe:



Help me buy back time by building structure around my work.



That means helping me:



* capture and prioritize tasks

* manage calendar and communications

* track commitments and follow-ups

* organize projects and initiatives

* convert recurring work into SOPs, checklists, templates, and workflows

* identify what should be delegated, automated, archived, or eliminated

* create cleaner operating systems for me and future support staff

* reduce friction, dropped balls, and decision fatigue



Over time, this may evolve into helping recruit, onboard, and coordinate additional VAs or operators using the systems we build together.





What success looks like:



Success in this role looks like:



* key tasks, deadlines, and follow-ups stop living in scattered notes, chats, and memory

* recurring work gets documented clearly enough for others to execute

* my calendar, inbox, and priorities become materially cleaner and easier to manage

* ideas get turned into organized projects with next actions

* the business develops a real internal operating rhythm

* more of my time is spent on high-value thinking, relationships, strategy, and revenue

* more of the company becomes scalable because execution is increasingly systemized



You are not here just to “help out.”



You are here to help build the operational backbone.





What you’ll be doing



Phase 1: Capture, audit, and organize



In the first phase, you’ll help me get a full picture of what exists and what matters.



This may include:



* auditing active tools, projects, subscriptions, and workflows

* organizing tasks, initiatives, and priorities into a real system

* helping clean up and structure Asana or whatever project management stack we finalize

* capturing founder context from voice notes, Looms, chats,
s, and ad hoc conversations

* identifying where time is being lost, where things get dropped, and where chaos is costing leverage



Phase 2: Distill and document



Once the moving pieces are clear, you’ll help convert them into usable systems.



This may include:



* building SOPs for recurring workflows

* creating checklists, templates, dashboards, and trackers

* documenting repeatable handoffs

* creating playbooks for future VAs or specialists

* helping define what should stay with me versus what should be delegated



Phase 3: Support scale



As structure improves, you may help support the next layer of growth.



This may include:



* helping recruit and screen future assistants or operators

* onboarding people into documented workflows

* managing follow-up and accountability across moving parts

* spotting opportunities for better automation, simplification, and process design

* helping create a calmer, more scalable operating cadence overall



Ongoing responsibilities



Ongoing work may include:



* inbox triage and draft support

* calendar management and scheduling

* task capture and follow-up tracking

* document and folder organization

* research and coordination tasks

* client, partner, or vendor follow-up support

* light project management

* turning messy inputs into clean outputs





Who this is for:



You’re likely a strong fit if:



* you genuinely enjoy organizing complexity

* you are calm, methodical, and highly proactive

* you can take incomplete, messy information and turn it into clear action

* you think in systems, not just tasks

* you naturally notice gaps, loose ends, and bottlenecks

* you communicate clearly in written and spoken English

* you are comfortable with a fast-moving founder who may jump between ideas quickly

* you like building SOPs, playbooks, checklists, and internal structure

* you are comfortable learning tools quickly

* you are interested in AI, automation, and better ways of working

* you can say, “Here’s what I captured, here’s what I organized, here’s what still needs a decision, and here’s my recommendation.”



Extra strong fit if you have experience with:



* executive assistant support

* founder support

* operations coordination

* SOP creation

* Asana, ClickUp, Notion, Airtable, Trello, or similar

* Google Workspace

* AI tools like ChatGPT

* supporting small but ambitious businesses





Who this is not for:



This role is probably not a fit if:



* you need extremely detailed instructions before you can begin

* you wait to be told every next step

* ambiguity makes you freeze

* you dislike fast-moving environments

* you see admin as “just completing tasks” rather than building systems

* you struggle with follow-through

* you are easily overwhelmed by changing priorities

* you are not comfortable with high standards



This role will energize the person who reads this and thinks:



“Finally. A role where I get to turn a brilliant mess into a machine.”





How I think about this role:



I strongly believe:



* almost any repeatable workflow can be documented

* almost anything documented can be delegated

* almost anything delegated can be improved

* and much of what is improved can eventually be automated



I also believe that real leverage comes from a combination of:



* good judgment

* strong systems

* clean communication

* and consistent follow-through



So I am not hiring “just an assistant.”



I am hiring someone who can help me create more leverage, more order, and more capacity.





Compensation, hours, and fit:



* Part-time or full-time, depending on fit

* Strong preference for full-time and long-term

* Compensation depends on experience and capability

* Please include your expected hourly rate

* You must have at least 4-hours per day of overlap with GMT+7 timezone



I care far more about:



* judgment

* communication

* organization

* ownership

* and long-term fit



than I do about finding the cheapest person.





How to apply



Please do not send a generic application.



I want to see how you think, how you communicate, and whether this role actually fits you.



Please send the following:





1) A short written intro



Include:



* your name

* where you’re based

* your relevant experience

* the kinds of founders or teams you’ve supported

* the tools you know well

* your expected hourly rate

* your weekly availability





2) A 2–3 minute audio or video recording



Please answer:



Why are you the right person to help a fast-moving founder organize chaos, build systems, and create a more scalable operation?



Also include:



* how you personally stay organized

* how you handle incomplete information

* how you turn messy recurring work into documented process

* what makes you different from a typical VA





3) A short written response to this scenario



A founder sends you:



* 17 scattered voice notes

* 43 WhatsApp messages

* 6 partially formed ideas

* 3 urgent follow-ups

* and says, “I need this organized.”



What do you do first? Walk me through your process.





4) One work sample



Please include one sample of something you created, such as:



* an SOP

* a checklist

* a dashboard

* a process document

* an operations tracker

* or a project-management system you helped build





5) Exact subject line



Use this exact subject line:



"Chaos to Clarity – [Your Name]"



Also include this exact phrase in the first line of your application:



"Systems create freedom."



Applications that miss either instruction will be ignored.





Final note:



I’m not looking for someone who can merely follow instructions.



I’m looking for someone who can capture them, improve them, document them, and eventually help others execute them well.



If this post makes you feel energized — not intimidated — there’s a good chance you’re the kind of person I want to hear from.



Let’s build real order around real capability.

title: iOS CarPlay Developer (Swift)
description:
Overview



We are building a mobile application called Convoy, focused on real-time group driving experiences (maps, convoy tracking, communication, and navigation).



We are looking for a senior iOS developer with CarPlay experience to design and implement our Apple CarPlay integration.



This is a focused, contract-based role working alongside a React Native team.



What You’ll Be Working On



- You will be responsible for building the CarPlay experience for our app, including:

- Displaying convoy routes and navigation on CarPlay

- Showing nearby convoy members in a driver-safe interface

- Handling real-time updates from the backend (location, status)

- Designing UI flows that comply with Apple CarPlay guidelines

- Integrating with an existing backend (Firebase / APIs)

- Collaborating with our React Native team to align data and behavior



Requirements (Must-Have)



- Strong experience with Swift and iOS development

- Experience building or contributing to Apple CarPlay applications

- Understanding of CarPlay UI templates and Apple restrictions

- Experience working with maps / navigation (MapKit or similar)

- Ability to consume APIs and handle real-time data updates

- Experience debugging and testing on real CarPlay environments



Nice to Have



- Experience bridging native iOS modules with React Native

- Familiarity with Firebase or similar backend services

- Experience with Android Auto (bonus, not required)



Engagement Details



- Full-time

- Project-based milestones (CarPlay MVP first)



What Success Looks Like



- CarPlay app launches and connects to Convoy backend

- Users can view convoy routes and basic session data

- Real-time updates work reliably

- UI complies with Apple’s CarPlay safety guidelines

- Clean, maintainable Swift code that integrates with the main app



Please include:



- Links or descriptions of any CarPlay apps or in-vehicle interfaces you have worked on.



If you have not worked with Apple CarPlay before, please do not apply.

title: Event Admin
description:
About Us

Event Strategies produces and owns a portfolio of B2B event properties across multiple industry sectors in Ireland and the UK. From flagship corporate conferences to curated industry gatherings, we deliver events that matter to the communities they serve. As we scale, we're looking for a sharp, organised Event Admin Executive who strives for the best to keep our event engine running smoothly behind the scenes.



The Role

You'll be the operational backbone of our event delivery team — managing the details that make the difference between a good event and a great one. Working closely with our Event Leads, you'll handle everything from entry processing and attendee coordination to post-event reporting, ensuring every programme runs without a hitch.

This is a remote role suited to someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, takes ownership of their work, and genuinely cares about getting things right.



What You'll Do

Event & Administrative Support

- Process entries, manage data, and maintain accurate records, including attendee lists and finalist databases

- Handle incoming event-related enquiries and communications professionally and promptly

- Proof and quality-check event materials before they go out



Attendee Services

- Manage guestlists and assist with registration processes

- Provide responsive online support to attendees and handle special requests



Team Coordination

- Report to Senior/Junior Event Leads and support the wider team as needed

- Liaise with Event Leads to coordinate the production of promotional materials

- Support the integration of multimedia elements into event presentations



Post-Event

- Gather feedback from attendees and vendors

- Assist in compiling post-event reports and performance analysis





What We're Looking For

- Experience in event coordination, administration, or a similar support role (a plus, not a must)

- Highly organised with strong multitasking ability

- Clear, confident communicator — written and verbal

- Comfortable with Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and event management tools

- Calm under pressure with a sharp eye for detail

- A self-starter who can manage their workload independently in a remote setting





What's on Offer

- Fully remote, full-time position

- Varied and engaging work across a diverse range of industry events

- A collaborative, supportive team environment, where attention to detail is valued above all else — if you've read this far, mention the word I love Events at the start of your

- Real opportunities to grow within the organisation





How to Apply

Send your CV and a brief cover letter outlining your relevant experience to 
 and 
 with the subject line "Event Admin Executive Application".

Event Strategies is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team.

title: Amazon Product Development and Sourcing Specialist
description:
Job Description:

As an Amazon Product Development and Sourcing Specialist, you will play a pivotal role in

identifying, developing, and sourcing products to enhance our presence on the Amazon

marketplace. This position involves extensive product research, collaboration with suppliers, and

managing the product development lifecycle.



Salary: $ 900 /month + 14 days paid leave after 3 months

Schedule: Full-time (9h/day)



Ideal Candidate Profile

?Analytical, organized, and detail-oriented

?Able to work independently with clear objectives and deadlines

?Logical thinker who understands the relationship between costs, demand, and profit

?Prior experience in e-commerce/Amazon FBA



Required Tools

?Helium 10 – strong knowledge (Cerebro, Black Box, Xray, Profits, Keyword Tracker)

?Google Sheets/Excel – formulas, tables, ROI calculations, dashboards

?Canva/PowerPoint - presentations

?Keepa – for price tracking and seasonality analysis

?Amazon Seller Central – basic knowledge to interpret sales data and BSR

?Alibaba & WeChat – for supplier research and communication

Jungle Scout – a plus



Required Skills

?Competitive analysis – ability to interpret data and make data-driven recommendations

?Financial knowledge – gross margin, logistics costs, profit estimation

?English – C2 Proficiency (Effective written and verbal communication)

?E-commerce experience



Key Responsibilities



- Product Research:

Conduct market research to identify high-potential products for Amazon (US/CA/AU/EU/UK/JP/SG marketplaces).

Analyze search volume, pricing, costs, and profitability (ROI, net margin, FBA fees).

Evaluate competition (customer reviews to inform product development decisions, pricing strategies, ratings, number of reviews, brand dominance).



- Track trends and identify emerging product niches.

? Collect, organize, and present data in Google Sheets/Excel/PowerPoint/Canva.

? Report weekly progress and suggest products with high potential.



- Product Development:

Work closely with cross-functional teams to conceptualize and develop new products that align with market demand and brand objectives.

Collaborate with design, marketing, and engineering teams to create innovative and

competitive products.



- Supplier Sourcing:

Identify and establish relationships with reliable suppliers and manufacturers for product sourcing.

Negotiate terms, pricing, and contracts with suppliers to ensure competitive advantage and optimal margins.



- Quality Assurance:

Develop and implement product quality standards in collaboration with suppliers.

Conduct regular quality inspections and audits to ensure products meet specified requirements and customer expectations.



- Cost Analysis:

Conduct cost analyses to determine product profitability, considering factors such as production costs, quality check, shipping, and all Amazon-related fees.



- Supply Chain Management:

Monitor and manage the end-to-end supply chain, ensuring timely production, shipping, and inventory replenishment.

Implement strategies to minimize lead times and optimize inventory levels.



- Compliance and Regulations:

? Stay informed about Amazon policies, regulations, and compliance standards.

? Ensure that products comply with all relevant safety and regulatory requirements.



- Data Analysis:

? Utilize data analytics tools to track product performance, sales trends, and customer feedback.

? Make data-driven decisions to optimize product listings and enhance overall sales

performance.



Qualifications:

? 3-5 years of experience as an Amazon product development and sourcing specialist

? Proven experience in product development, sourcing, and supply chain management, preferably within the e-commerce or retail industry.

? Strong negotiation and communication skills.

? Familiarity with Amazon marketplace policies and procedures.

? Analytical mindset with proficiency in data analysis tools.

? Detail-oriented with a commitment to ensuring product quality and customer satisfaction.



About Us

We are BTS Brands, a dynamic company with a diverse portfolio of products and a strong commitment to growth. Since 2018, we’ve been successfully selling on Amazon and proudly own two brands in the travel and beauty industries.

As we continue to expand, we’re looking for passionate, dedicated individuals to join our team.

At BTS Brands, we foster a positive and supportive work environment where everyone can thrive, develop their skills, and make a meaningful impact. We offer competitive compensation, opportunities for career advancement, and the chance to

collaborate with a team that shares your passion for excellence. If you’re ready to take your career to the next level and grow with a company committed to innovation and quality, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Logistics & Supply Chain Lead
description:
Job Title Logistics & Supply Chain Lead

Department Logistics

Work Arrangement Fully Remote

Hours Company operates on EST hours; flexible scheduling available

Reports To CEO



COMPANY SNAPSHOT

We’re a growing e-commerce brand focused on bedding and textile products, selling primarily through Amazon. Our catalog spans approximately 20 product lines with deep variation across colors and sizes. We’re in a high-growth phase with strong product-market fit, and we’re building the team to scale significantly over the next 12 months.



WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

Our logistics department has more work than bandwidth. Recurring operations, analytical projects, and new process development all compete for the same limited hours, and two of our four strategic pillars—Supply Chain & Supplier Management and 3PL & FBA Operations—have minimal formal coverage today.

Without this hire, significant financial exposure in storage fees and surcharges continues to compound, supplier negotiation and freight optimization remain unaddressed, and the forecasting process can’t mature into a cross-functional system. This role exists to take ownership of processes and planning so the department can operate across all four pillars simultaneously—and so the financial leaks stop growing.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

•Inventory Intelligence & Demand Planning — Own days-of-supply targets, safety stock methodology, and the forecasting process across all SKU tiers. Keep hero SKUs within target supply windows. Build a cross-functional forecasting process that pulls from PPC, deals, and channel data to continuously improve accuracy.

•Supply Chain & Supplier Management — Build this function from the ground up. Establish the PO lifecycle from issuance to receipt, create supplier scorecards tracking lead time, defect rates, and cost per unit, coordinate QC, and manage freight. Define what good looks like and build toward it.

•3PL & FBA Operations — Manage 3PL relationships and FBA inbound logistics. Define and track 3PL SLAs, plan inbound shipments to avoid surcharge threshold breaches, build a reimbursement recovery process, and monitor FC check-in and receiving times.

•Cost Optimization & Storage Governance — Drive systematic fee mitigation across long-term storage fees, storage utilization surcharges, shipping rates, and landed cost. Own the financial exposure dashboard. Make sure we never get surprised by a cost cliff we should have seen coming.

•Team Development & Process Formalization — Document SOPs for all recurring processes, establish cadences that run without prompting, cross-train to eliminate single points of failure, and develop the team as it grows. Build a department that works even when you’re not in the room.



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In the first 90 days:

•You’re independently running the weekly KPI cadence and department check-in

•You’ve taken ownership of the Supply Chain and 3PL pillars, with current-state processes documented and first improvements underway

•Storage fee exposure and surcharge risk are under active, systematic management—not reactive firefighting

By 12 months:

•All four strategic pillars are staffed and operating with documented SOPs

•KPIs are trending positively across days of supply, storage fees, surcharges, forecast accuracy, and landed cost

•The department can absorb demand fluctuations without crisis-mode escalation, and leadership oversight has shifted from weekly hands-on involvement to monthly strategic reviews



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Requirements:

•Strong proficiency in written and spoken English

•4–6 years in logistics or supply chain roles, with at least 2 years owning outcomes (not just executing tasks) in a leadership or senior IC capacity

•Hands-on Amazon FBA experience—you understand long-term storage fee mechanics, storage utilization surcharges, inbound shipment planning, and fee structures at a tactical level

•Experience managing at least one 3PL relationship end-to-end (contracts, SLAs, cost benchmarking, receiving coordination)

•Proven track record of building processes from scratch in a resource-constrained environment—not just optimizing what someone else built

•Experience managing or building a small logistics team—you’ll be leading existing operators and growing the function over time

•Advanced Excel / Google Sheets fluency—this is a spreadsheet-heavy environment and you’ll be building models, dashboards, and trackers from day one

•Financial acuity—you naturally translate logistics decisions into dollar impact and use that to drive prioritization

You’ll thrive here if you:

•Treat the department’s financial exposure like it’s your own money—you don’t wait to be told something is a problem

•Instinctively create repeatable processes rather than solving the same problem manually each time

•Can look at a list of urgent items and pick the ones that actually matter this week, then communicate clearly about what’s being deferred and why



THIS ISN’T FOR YOU IF…

•Your strength is strategy and frameworks but you’re not excited about personally pulling data and building spreadsheets—at least until the team grows

•You need heavy top-down direction to know what to work on, or you need perfect information before you’re willing to make a call

•Your experience is entirely in enterprise or Fortune 500 logistics without e-commerce or Amazon exposure

•You see a small, early-stage team and think “that’s beneath me” rather than “that’s a chance to build something”



WHAT WE OFFER

Work Arrangement Fully Remote. Company operates on EST hours with flexible scheduling.

Growth Opportunity This is a ground-floor leadership role. You’ll build the logistics function and grow the team beneath you. As the operation scales, so does your scope and impact.

Team & Culture You’ll join a lean, high-autonomy team where results matter more than process theater. The CEO provides strategic direction and removes structural blockers; you run the operation. Expect direct communication, low ego, and a bias toward action.



HOW TO APPLY

To apply, submit your resume to hiring team 
 along with a brief response (3–5 sentences) to the following question:

Describe a time you inherited a logistics or supply chain function with no documented processes and built the operating system from scratch. What did you prioritize first, and why?



This question is your first filter. Candidates who can answer it with specifics—not generalities—are the ones we want to talk to.



If this sounds like the kind of challenge you’re built for, we’d like to hear from you.

title: Real Estate Cold Caller (Outbound Sales – High Volume Dialing)
description:
If you’ve done 100+ calls/day, read this



Cold Caller (Outbound Sales)



Salary: $700–$800 USD per month (Full-Time, Remote)



Schedule (Important):



Thursday–Monday required

Must work 1pm–9pm EST (weekdays) and 10am–6pm EST (weekends)

40 hours/week

Day off: Tuesday or Wednesday



We are actively building a high volume off market pipeline.



Over the last 6 months:



Generated
+ leads.

Consistently increasing seller conversations month over month

Building a structured pipeline across multiple NJ counties



We are looking for an energetic Cold Calling Specialist to join our real estate acquisitions team. In this role, you will make a high volume of outbound calls to homeowners and gauge their interest in selling their property. 



Your daily target will be ~150 calls (and up to 250–300 calls on days we use a multi-line dialer). You’ll follow a proven script to identify motivated sellers, handle initial objections, and transfer hot leads to our acquisitions team. This is a key role that requires excellent spoken English and a friendly, persistent attitude.



Responsibilities:



High-Volume Outbound Calling: Make at least 150 calls per day to targeted property owners using our click-to-dial system. 

(On certain campaigns with a multi-line dialer, you may dial 250–300 numbers a day.) Maintain a positive, professional demeanor throughout all calls.





Lead Qualification: 

Follow our prepared script to introduce our company and ask key questions about the homeowner’s interest in selling. Listen actively to identify motivation, timeline, and property details.





Overcome Objections: 

Professionally handle common pushbacks (“Not interested,” “How did you get my number?” etc.) with polite and confident responses. Stay resilient and upbeat even after repeated rejections – it often takes dozens of “no’s” to get a “yes.”





Record Keeping: 

Update call outcomes in our CRM in real time. Mark leads as interested, not interested, wrong number, etc., and add any relevant notes from the conversation. Accuracy in tagging call results is crucial for follow-up.





Target Achievement: 

Strive to meet or exceed weekly and monthly goals, such as number of leads (interested sellers) generated, appointments set, or transfers to the sales team. Work with the team to continuously improve performance (e.g. refining the script or call approach based on feedback).





Requirements:

Excellent English & Neutral Accent: Fluent spoken English with a neutral or minimal accent. You should be easily understood by American homeowners. (We will request a voice recording or conduct a phone screening to verify this.)





Cold Calling Experience: 

At least 1 year of experience in outbound calling/telesales, telemarketing, or a related role. Experience calling US-based customers or real estate cold calling is a big plus.





Resilience & Energy: 

Positive attitude and ability to handle rejection. You can make hundreds of calls and still keep an enthusiastic tone. Self-motivated and driven to reach targets.





Tech Requirements: 

Comfortable using auto-dialer software and CRM systems. (Experience with tools like Mojo Dialer or CallTools, and Podio or a similar CRM, is helpful.) Must have a reliable high-speed internet connection, a quality headset, and a quiet workspace for making calls.





Schedule: 

Availability to work U.S. business hours (e.g. 9am–5pm Eastern or Central Time). This may be overnight if you live in a different timezone – please ensure you can commit to these hours long-term.





What We Offer:

Competitive Base Pay + Bonus: Base salary of $700–$800/month, with performance bonuses for each deal closed from the leads you generate (we love to reward results!).





Training & Support: 

Ongoing training in effective cold calling techniques, real estate knowledge, and scripts. You’ll have team support and coaching to help you succeed.





Growth Opportunities: 

This is a long-term position. Top performers can take on greater responsibilities over time or grow into other roles (e.g. Lead Manager or Team Lead).





Team Culture: 

Join a friendly, results-driven team. We celebrate wins and learn from losses together. Your contributions will be valued as an important part of our acquisitions process.





Remote Work: 

Work from home and save commute time. We just ask for reliable attendance and communication during work hours.





How to Apply: 





To be considered, please send your resume and a voice recording (1-2 minutes) introducing yourself. In the recording, briefly tell us about your cold calling experience or read a few lines from a script to showcase your accent and energy. You may use a free service like Vocaroo and share the link. 



Applications without a voice sample will not be reviewed, since speaking ability is crucial for this role. We will invite promising candidates for a live interview and mock call. 



Excited to hear from you, let’s hit those dialing goals together!

 Post, Cold Caller Position



$700-$800 USD/month + bonuses 

(Full-Time, Remote)

We’re looking for an outbound cold calling pro to join our real estate team! 

If you have great English (neutral accent) and aren’t afraid to make 150+ dials a day, this job is for you. 



Help us connect with homeowners and find those interested in selling.



Key Tasks:

Make
calls daily using our auto dialer, we provide the leads and script.





Talk to homeowners, follow the script to introduce our home-buying service.





Handle initial “Not interested” objections with a smile and keep the convo going.





Mark call results in our CRM (interested vs. not interested, etc.) and send hot leads to our team.





We’re Looking For:

Excellent English speaker with a neutral/minimal accent (you’ll be talking to Americans).





Cold calling experience, you know how to engage strangers on the phone and stay upbeat despite rejections.





Resilient & positive attitude,  can you hear “no” 100 times and still dial the next number enthusiastically? That’s what we need!





Tech ready: 

Good internet, quality headset, and a quiet place to call from.





What You Get:

Base pay + bonuses for deals closed from your leads~~~ your efforts are handsomely rewarded!





Training and scripts provided, we set you up for success. Be part of the family.





Long term career with a supportive team (we love to promote our best people!).





Work from home, save time and money, just be available on US hours.





Sound like a fit? 



Comment below or D
 your CV and a voice clip of you saying hello! 



(We’ll listen for that clear accent.) 







Opportunity to start ASAP, let’s crush some calls together!

title: Bookkeeper (Required to have an AU experience)
description:
Role Purpose

The Bookkeeper is responsible for the end-to-end delivery of core transactional finance processes, including accounts payable, bank reconciliations, corporate cards, payroll processing, and financial data administration. This role is critical to the smooth running of the finance function, ensuring accurate, timely, high-quality financial processing that supports strong reporting, compliance, and organizational decision-making.



Role Responsibilities

A. Accounts Payable:

Process all supplier invoices, employee & volunteer expense claims, ensuring correct coding, approvals, GST treatment, currency, and documentation.

Manage the AP inbox and triage queries.

Maintain accurate supplier records, including bank details and documentation checks.

Prepare AP batches in line with cut-off timelines and approval workflows.

Conduct first-pass AP review before Management Accountant review (accuracy, completeness, coding).

Reconcile AP accounts and investigate variances.



B. Accounts Receivable:

Prepare and send invoices to partners, funders, and internal stakeholders.

Maintain AR schedules and follow up on outstanding amounts.

Reconcile AR balances and ensure all receipts are allocated.



C. Corporate Credit Cards:

Manage card issuance, cancellations, limits, and user onboarding.

Review monthly card claims, ensuring receipts, coding, and notes are provided.

Follow up late claims and unresolved discrepancies.

Reconcile company card accounts and ensure accurate EOM processing.



D. Payroll:

Prepare and process fortnightly payroll in line with contracts, and organisational policies.

Maintain employee master data with Xero, ensuring accuracy of bank details, tax information, superannuation, and entitlements.

Process new starters, changes, terminations, and pay adjustments.

Reconcile payroll reports, checking for variance and anomalies.

Prepare workpapers for superannuation payments, payroll journals, and leave reconciliations.

Work with P&E to ensure payroll accuracy, and timely updates to employee records.



E. Bank Reconciliation:

Complete daily and monthly bank reconciliations across all accounts.

Investigate and resolve unreconciled transactions.

Ensure all receipts, payments, and transfers are accurately recorded.



F. End of Month Processes:

Draft prepayments, accruals, apportionments, and routine journals.

Prepare EOM workpapers to a standard ready for first review.

Ensure all transactional processing is complete before close deadlines.

Maintain high-quality data hygiene to support forecasting, reporting, and analysis.



G. Data Entry and Record Keeping:

Maintain accurate, organised financial files and documentation for audit readiness.

Ensure data entry is correct, complete, and aligned with financial policies and accounting standards.

Support system updates, automation improvements, and process changes.



H. General Administrative Support:

Provide customer-focused support to internal stakeholders on finance and payroll queries.

Assist the Finance Team during peak periods (audit, EOFY, EOM, etc).

Participate in continuous improvement projects.

Contribute to team discussions on process optimisation, automation, and better ways of working.



Key Skills and Experience

-Experience in a finance support, accounts, or payroll role.

-Solid understanding of accounts payable, accounts receivable, and payroll processes.

-Proficient with Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets.

-Experience with accounting software (Xero preferred).

-Strong numeracy and data-entry accuracy.

-Understanding of basic accounting and payroll principles.

-High attention to detail and ability to deliver consistent accuracy.

-Excellent time management and ability to work to tight deadlines.

-Strong communication and customer service skills.

-Ability to work independently and take ownership of tasks.

-Comfortable with change, learning, and improving processes.

-Ability to handle confidential information with integrity and discretion.

-Certificate, Diploma, or Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, or related field

title: Hiring 4 Tax Accountants (AU)
description:
We believe our people are the foundation of our success. Our culture is built on respect, collaboration, and supporting one another.



We strive to create a workplace where employees feel valued, heard, and empowered to grow both personally and professionally. Our leadership is committed to fostering open communication, professional development, and a positive environment where everyone can perform at their best.



If you're looking for a company that values your contribution and supports your growth, we’d love to have you on our team.



Why Join Bravo?

We believe great people deserve great support. Here’s what you can expect when you join us:

-Competitive and stable full-time work from home opportunity

-HMO and Life Insurance coverage

-13th Month Pay

-Government Contributions (SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG)

-Sick Leave and Vacation Leave benefits

-Free Training and Professional Development opportunities

-Monthly Virtual Employee Engagement activities (with prizes!)

-Fun Company Get-Togethers

-An Annual Christmas Party that our team truly looks forward to

-Year-End Package



We make sure our team not only works hard, but also feels appreciated and celebrated.



About the Role

We are currently looking for four (4) experienced AU Tax Accountants to join our growing team.

In this role, you will work closely with an Australian firm handling tax compliance and accounting services for a diverse client base. This position offers the opportunity to further develop your expertise in Australian taxation while working in a supportive and collaborative environment.



Key Responsibilities

-Prepare and review Australian Tax Returns, BAS, and Financial Statements

-Ensure compliance with Australian tax regulations and legislation

-Provide tax planning support or advice to clients or internal stakeholders

-Analyze financial statements and provide insights where needed

-Maintain accurate financial records and documentation



What We’re Looking For

-2–5+ years of Australian Tax and Business Services experience

-Ability to prepare tax returns for Individuals, Partnerships, Companies (including Group Companies), and Trusts

-Experience in preparing and reviewing Financial Statements

-Solid understanding of Australian tax legislation, including:

Capital Gains Tax (CGT)

Fringe Benefits Tax (FBT)

Division 7A compliance and application

-Experience in preparing IAS and BAS

-Experience in tax planning is an advantage

-Strong analytical and communication skills

-Proficient in using Xero, XPM, and other accounting software

-Positive and proactive attitude with a fun team spirit

-Bookkeeping experience is a plus



We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Administrative Coordinator
description:
Local Foreigner is a boutique consultancy specializing in high-end curated travel. From the bespoke to the basics, we guide our clients on everything from a weekend in Paris to an expedition to Patagonia. Our mission is to deliver highly personalized travel experiences through thoughtful service and a seamless planning process. 



We are seeking a detail-oriented and resourceful Administrative Coordinator to support day-to-day operations. This role requires strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.  



This is a unique opportunity to join a small, collaborative team in a supportive but demanding work environment. 



COMPENSATION 



Training period (4–6 months): $5/hour + monthly internet allowance 

Post-training: $6/hour + monthly internet allowance 



WORK SCHEDULE 



Full-time (40 hours per week) 

Typical schedule: 9 PM – 5 AM Philippine local time (aligned with EU/US hours) 

Flexibility to support US weekend hours is a plus 



RESPONSIBILITIES 



All Administrative tea
mbers are cross-trained and support a range of operational and travel support-related tasks, including: 

Responding to Help Desk tickets for internal and affiliate teams 

Managing shared inbox and phone inquiries 

Scheduling supplier meetings  

Supporting recruitment lifecycle including corresponding with candidates and arranging interviews 

Updating internal Salesforce database (contacts, suppliers, and other related materials) 

Supporting the A/R collection processes 

Creating and updating client travel documents (itineraries, rate checks) 

Drafting and updating SOPs and other internal documentation 

Providing administrative support to partners and senior leadership 

Assisting with ad-hoc projects as needed 



REQUIREMENTS 



Proficient in Microsoft Office; Confluence, Jira, Salesforce and QuickBooks experience is a plus 

Experience in an administrative, virtual assistant, or similar support role 

Strong attention to detail and organizational skills 

Proactive, resourceful, and able to problem-solve independently 

Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines 

Strong written and verbal communication skills 

Team player with the ability to work independently 

Previous experience in a travel support or travel operations role is a plus 

Experience with Sabre or other GDS systems is a plus

title: Remote Bilingual Customer Support (Thai/English) - Music Streaming
description:
We Could Use Someone Like You in Our Crew.

Are you passionate about music and fluent in both Thai and English? ModSquad is excited to partner with a leading global music streaming platform, and we're on the lookout for friendly, tech-savvy Customer Service Representatives to join our all-remote team!



As a member of this team, you will be the first point of contact for customers, helping them navigate the platform, troubleshoot technical issues, and ensuring they have the best possible listening experience. Specifically, you will be responsible for responding to customer inquiries via inbound messages in both Thai and English.



Important  note: All qualified applicants will have to complete language assessments in Thai and English.

 



Orientation Hours (Pacific Time):

 



40 hours per week are required for the 8-week orientation period.



Orientation phase 1: 4 weeks, 4 AM - 1PM

Orientation phase 2: 4 weeks, self-scheduled between the hours of 6 AM - 7 PM



Available Production Hours (Pacific Time): 



Daily, 6 AM - 7 PM

 

Project Commitments: 



25 hours per week

12 hours are required on Saturday and/or Sunday

180 days (as needed by ModSquad)

 

Need help figuring out the time difference? Check out World Time Buddy! Just set Sacramento, California as your starting point, then add your location to easily convert Orientation, Nesting, and Production hours to your local time.



Who Are You?



You’re passionate about creating personalized, memorable experiences for every customer.

You thrive on delivering top-tier customer support and can swiftly spot and solve any issues that come your way.

You’ve got experience providing support through inbound messaging and know how to keep cool under pressure.

You've handled 3-5 simultaneous chats in previous roles.

You're fluent in Thai and English, with professional-level communication skills in both.



Workspace Requirements:



A dedicated laptop or desktop computer running Windows 10 or newer

Willingness to install ModSquad security software and a 2FA app on your mobile device

Access to a webcam or a smartphone capable of capturing clear photos

A stable broadband internet connection with a minimum speed of 25 Mbps

At least 8 GB of RAM (more is always better!)

Dual monitors are strongly recommended for optimal workflow and multitasking

Please note: A Chromebook is not sufficient for ModSquad projects.



Application link - 



About ModSquad: 

 



ModSquad has been reinventing the Customer Experience Services industry since 2007. Top brands around the globe turn to us for customer support, content moderation, trust and safety, community management, and social media services. We work with startups and Fortune 500 companies and everything in between. Our client list includes Spotify, VSCO, Vimeo, Tourism Ireland, and a ton of other companies we aren’t at liberty to talk about. We support over 50 languages in more than 90 countries. We’re primarily a remote company so you’ve already seen our/your office. If you want to work with great people on cool projects for amazing brands, you’ve come to the right place.

 



ModSquad is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), gender, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, military status, status as a veteran or disabled veteran, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital or family status, genetic information, medical condition, or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state, or local law, ordinance, or regulation.
